title: Visiting the Dentist
conversation: Tina visits her dentist, Dr. Li, for a checkup. After taking X-rays, Dr. Li tells Tina she'll need to replace a filling. Dr. Li: Have your teeth been giving you any trouble since your last checkup? Tina: The back molar on the left has been killing me. Dr. Li: Let's see here. It looks like the filling is loose. Tina: Isn't that the same one you replaced last year? Dr. Li: I'll need to check. I can't remember off the top of my head. Tina: Also, one of my top right teeth is a little sensitive. Dr. Li: Which one? Tina: This one. Dr. Li: Okay, I'll take a look. It looks a little discolored. We'll get a set of X-rays today to get to the bottom of it. Tina: X-rays? Will that be an out-of-pocket expense? Dr. Li: No, you're due for a set. It'll be covered by your insurance. (Dr. Li takes the X-rays.) Dr. Li: (looking at X-rays) Let me give these a once over . . . It looks like we can hold off on that top tooth. Tina: It does hurt, but maybe it's just a figment of my imagination! Dr. Li: Call us if it keeps acting up. In the meantime, we'll need to replace that filling. Please make an appointment for that.

idioms.meaning_en: To cause pain, difficulty, or problems for someone
idioms.meaning_vi: gây rắc rối, làm đau hoặc gây khó khăn
idioms.example: My old back injury has been giving me trouble all week.
idioms.context: Have your teeth been giving you any trouble since your last checkup?

idioms.meaning_en: To cause extreme pain or discomfort
idioms.meaning_vi: đau điếng, đau không chịu nổi
idioms.example: My feet are killing me after standing for eight hours.
idioms.context: The back molar on the left has been killing me.

idioms.idiom: off the top of my head
idioms.meaning_en: From memory, without checking facts or records
idioms.meaning_vi: nhớ mang máng, không cần kiểm tra lại
idioms.example: I can't tell you the price off the top of my head, but I'll look it up.
idioms.context: I can't remember off the top of my head.

idioms.idiom: get to the bottom of it
idioms.meaning_en: To find the true cause or source of a problem
idioms.meaning_vi: tìm ra ngọn ngành, tìm hiểu rõ nguyên nhân
idioms.example: We need to get to the bottom of why the computer keeps crashing.
idioms.context: We'll get a set of X-rays today to get to the bottom of it.

idioms.meaning_en: An expense paid with one's own money, often to be reimbursed later
idioms.meaning_vi: tiền túi, chi phí tự túc
idioms.example: I had to pay for the hotel out-of-pocket because I lost my company card.
idioms.context: Will that be an out-of-pocket expense?

idioms.meaning_en: Ready for or needing something at a scheduled or expected time
idioms.meaning_vi: đến lúc phải làm gì, đến kỳ hạn
idioms.example: Your car is due for an oil change.
idioms.context: No, you're due for a set.

idioms.meaning_en: To be paid for by insurance or a specific policy
idioms.meaning_vi: được chi trả bởi, được bảo hiểm chi trả
idioms.example: Is dental surgery covered by your health insurance?
idioms.context: It'll be covered by your insurance.

idioms.meaning_en: To examine or look at something quickly
idioms.meaning_vi: kiểm tra sơ bộ, nhìn lướt qua
idioms.example: Let me give your essay a once over before you turn it in.
idioms.context: Let me give these a once over . . .

idioms.meaning_en: To delay or wait before doing something
idioms.example: We should hold off on buying the house until the market improves.
idioms.context: It looks like we can hold off on that top tooth.

idioms.meaning_en: Something that is not real and exists only in the mind
idioms.meaning_vi: điều do tưởng tượng ra, không có thật
idioms.example: The monster under the bed was just a figment of my imagination.
idioms.context: It does hurt, but maybe it's just a figment of my imagination!

idioms.meaning_en: To function improperly or cause pain again
idioms.example: My printer is acting up and won't connect to the Wi-Fi.
idioms.context: Call us if it keeps acting up.

idioms.meaning_en: During the period of time between two events
idioms.meaning_vi: trong khi chờ đợi, trong lúc đó
idioms.example: The pizza will be here in 20 minutes; in the meantime, let's watch TV.
idioms.context: In the meantime, we'll need to replace that filling.

idioms.meaning_en: To arrange a specific time and date for a meeting
idioms.example: I need to call the salon to make an appointment for a haircut.
idioms.context: Please make an appointment for that.
